events.change_summary: Intent check: incomplete path display, false-positive loss warnings, smarter path completion

events.summary: Intent check pushed: three focused improvements — fix false-positive mid-path loss warnings, surface stall location in incomplete paths, and improve path completion with smarter IP selection and TCP probing. Advanced metrics (jitter, PMTU, IPv6) are out of scope.

events.change_summary: Expand scope to full swiss army knife vision: all CCIE-grade metrics plus path accuracy fixes

events.summary: Revised intent check: expanded to full swiss army knife vision covering all CCIE-grade metrics (ECMP tracing, jitter, PMTU, TCP/TLS latency, IPv6, loss confidence, route stability, IXP classification) plus the two accuracy bug fixes and path completion improvements.

events.change_summary: Full scope: 16 requirements across 3 phases covering bug fixes, smarter probing, and 9 new CCIE-grade path metrics

events.rationale: backend-feature: CLI tool — all display changes are terminal output additions following established Rich patterns, no design-stage thinking required. Architecture included: task touches 5+ existing modules (cli.py, diagnosis.py, display.py, mtr.py, country.py) and introduces 2 new modules (pmtu.py, ixp.py), warranting a dedicated architecture pass before implementation. Tier full: 16 requirements across 10+ files, new subsystems, cross-cutting changes.

alternatives_considered: Flag suspect hops in mtr.py during parse and let diagnosis.py trust the flag
Suppress the verdict only when path_complete=True (i.e., destination was reached)
events.decision: ICMP rate-limit false positive fixed via forward-scan in diagnosis.py, not by adding a per-hop flag in mtr.py
events.rationale: diagnosis.py is a pure function with no I/O — the fix belongs there. Adding a rate_limited flag to the mtr hub dict would couple transport to diagnosis concerns. The forward-scan (check whether all subsequent responsive hops have Loss% ≤ 1.0 after finding a candidate mid-path loss hop) is a one-pass O(n) addition that keeps diagnosis.py self-contained.

alternatives_considered: scapy for PMTU probing (would give raw ICMP but requires root and adds a heavy dep)
httpx for PeeringDB fetch (requests already present)
cryptography library for TLS introspection (standard ssl sufficient for handshake timing)
events.decision: No new PyPI runtime dependencies in any phase
events.rationale: PMTU probing uses subprocess ping (already used in cli.py), TCP/TLS latency uses standard socket+ssl modules, IXP classification uses requests (already a runtime dep). Keeping the dependency surface flat reduces install friction and maintains the tool's uvx-installable zero-config nature.

alternatives_considered: Fetch on every classify_hop() call (too slow for country sweeps)
Bundle a static IXP prefix list in the package (would become stale)
events.decision: IXP prefix data cached in process memory, not on disk
events.rationale: netpath is a CLI tool that exits after each run. Disk caching would require a cache invalidation strategy and a writable path. In-process caching is sufficient: country mode sweeps call classify_hop() many times within a single run, so one fetch per process amortizes the cost without adding persistence complexity.

events.summary: Full scope written as revision 2: 16 requirements across 3 phases (bug fixes → smarter probing + calibrated metrics → advanced path analysis), 14 acceptance criteria, 3 decisions logged. Workflow: backend-feature, full tier, architecture stage included before implementation.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.change_summary: Surfaced 4 open decisions: PMTU probe mechanism, multi-pass opt-in vs always-on, probe count sourcing for loss calibration, and latency measurement module placement.

events.summary: Surfaced 4 open decisions covering PMTU probe privilege model, multi-pass tracing opt-in vs always-on, probe count sourcing for loss calibration, and latency module placement. Awaiting builder input before writing the full architecture.

events.change_summary: Full architecture with phase numbers added: 3 phases, 4 new files, 6 modified modules, complete scorecard and verification steps

events.decision: AD-1: mtr.run() returns list[dict] when passes=1 and list[list[dict]] when passes>1, rather than a separate run_multi() function
events.rationale: The requirement specifies mtr.run() must accept a passes parameter, and callers already gate on the passes value to decide how to interpret results. A single function matches the spec and avoids a parallel API surface.

events.decision: AD-2: prefer_tcp parameter added to mtr.run_traceroute() rather than exposing _run_traceroute_cmd directly from cli.py
events.rationale: All subprocess management is encapsulated in mtr.py per project convention. Calling a private _run_traceroute_cmd from cli.py would break the module boundary. Adding prefer_tcp=False keeps the change backward-compatible and locates the TCP-first logic alongside the existing UDP fallback.

events.decision: AD-3: probe_count in result dict is set to cycles (the mtr cycles parameter) in _measure(), requiring no new CLI parameter
events.rationale: The mtr cycles parameter already represents the number of probe packets sent per hop. Threading it through _measure() as probe_count satisfies the calibrated loss requirement without surfacing a new --probe-count flag or changing the CLI contract.

events.decision: AD-4: TCP/TLS latency lives in a new src/netpath/latency.py module, not inline in cli.py
events.rationale: The project follows single-purpose file conventions (iperf.py, speedtest.py, rum.py each own one measurement concern). A dedicated latency.py is independently testable with no subprocess mocking required, and matches the pattern used for the other two new Phase 3 modules.

events.summary: Full architecture complete: 3 phases, 4 new files (pmtu.py, latency.py, ixp.py, test_country.py), 6 modified source files, 4 architecture decisions logged. Phase 1 is Small, Phase 2 Medium, Phase 3 Large. All 16 requirements and 14 acceptance criteria are covered with concrete per-phase verification steps.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Starting Phase 1: Fix the two accuracy bugs

events.description: Starting phase 1: Fix the two accuracy bugs

events.details: 15 passed (6 diagnosis, 8 mtr, 1 new rate_limited_hop test). Pre-existing test_mid_path_packet_loss updated to reflect genuine congestion semantics (downstream hop also lossy). ruff clean.

alternatives_considered: Compute stall_hop in a separate call in _measure() after _classify_path returns
events.decision: Implementation: _classify_path() computes stall_hop and last rtt_ms during the incomplete-path scan pass (not a separate pass) and overrides last_rtt_ms in _measure() only when classification finds a better value
events.rationale: Avoids a second iteration over hubs. The incomplete branch already iterates hubs once to find entry_transit_asn; tracking stall_hop and rtt_ms in the same loop is O(n) and keeps the data co-located with other path classification logic.

alternatives_considered: Add a second test for the new behavior instead of updating the old one
Mark the old test as xfail
events.decision: Implementation: existing test_mid_path_packet_loss updated to use a downstream-lossy hub (8% at hop 3) to represent genuine congestion after the rate-limit fix changed the semantics of the old 3-hop all-clean-downstream case
events.rationale: The original test had hop 2 lossy and hop 3 clean — exactly the rate-limited pattern the new forward-scan suppresses. The test was asserting pre-fix behavior that is now correctly reclassified as Healthy. Updated to a case where downstream loss confirms congestion.

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: Fix the two accuracy bugs

events.summary: Fixed ICMP rate-limit false positive in diagnosis.py via forward-scan; added stall_hop to _classify_path and threaded it through _measure; country_summary incomplete rows now show stall ASN, hop number, and RTT.

items.description: Run `uv run python3 -m pytest tests/test_diagnosis.py::test_rate_limited_hop_produces_healthy_not_mid_path_loss -v` and confirm it passes with verdict Healthy and a `rate_limited_hops` signal.
Run `uv run python3 -m pytest tests/ -v` and confirm all 15 tests pass with no failures.
Run `uv run python3 -m ruff check .` and confirm it exits with `All checks passed!`.

Run `netpath country US --no-throughput` against a country with known incomplete paths and confirm incomplete rows now display `⚠ stalled at AS{n}, hop {n}, {rtt} ms` instead of the flat `⚠ incomplete` label. (Requires mtr installed and network access.)
For a path that does complete end-to-end, verify the country summary still shows the verified RTT in the grouped complete-paths section with no stall context shown.

events.note: Starting Phase 2: Smarter probing and calibrated metrics

events.description: Starting phase 2: Smarter probing and calibrated metrics

events.details: 22 passed (7 diagnosis, 8 mtr, 3 country, 4 new Phase 2 tests). All ruff checks clean.

alternatives_considered: Keep 1.0 hardcoded in the forward-scan and adjust the test to not have downstream loss
Use a separate constant for the rate-limit suppression cutoff
events.decision: Implementation: forward-scan "clean downstream" comparison updated to use loss_threshold instead of hardcoded 1.0
events.rationale: With calibrated thresholds, the rate-limit suppression logic must use the same threshold as the trigger. If probe_count=100 gives threshold=0.5%, a downstream hop at 0.8% is an alarm — not a clean signal. Using loss_threshold in both the trigger and the forward-scan keeps the semantics consistent: a hop is rate-limiting only when downstream is genuinely below the alarm level for the current sample size.

events.change_summary: Phase 2 complete: Smarter probing and calibrated metrics

events.summary: Country mode now selects test IPs from the most-specific announced prefix and prefers TCP-443 traceroute when no iperf3 server is available. _measure() computes jitter_ms (mean StDev across responsive hubs) and sets probe_count=cycles. diagnosis.py gains calibrated loss thresholds (5%/1%/0.5% by sample size), a High Jitter warning at 10ms, and consistent use of loss_threshold in the rate-limit forward-scan. JSON output includes jitter_ms. 22/22 tests pass, ruff clean.

items.description: Run `uv run python -m pytest tests/test_country.py -v` and confirm all 3 country tests pass.

Run `uv run python -m pytest tests/test_diagnosis.py -v` and confirm all 11 diagnosis tests pass, including the 4 new Phase 2 tests (`test_high_jitter_warning`, `test_jitter_below_threshold_is_healthy`, `test_calibrated_loss_few_probes_no_alarm`, `test_calibrated_loss_many_probes_strict_threshold`).
Run `uv run python -m pytest tests/ -v` and confirm all 22 tests pass with no failures.
Run `uv run python -m ruff check .` and confirm it exits with `All checks passed!`.

Run `netpath asn AS15169 --json --no-throughput` and confirm the output JSON contains a `jitter_ms` field (numeric or null). Example check: `netpath asn AS15169 --json --no-throughput | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('jitter_ms' in d)"` should print `True`.

Run `netpath country US --no-throughput --top 3` and confirm that for ASNs without an iperf3 server, the path trace completes or shows a stall location rather than a flat failure. (Requires mtr installed and network access — a TCP-443 traceroute attempt should be visible in the output.)

events.note: Starting Phase 3: Advanced path analysis — pmtu.py, latency.py, ixp.py, mtr multi-pass, ECMP, IPv6 dual-stack, IXP labels, and 4 new diagnosis signals

events.description: Implementing core changes — new modules, mtr multi-pass, diagnosis signals, display IXP labels

events.details: 36 passed (14 diagnosis including 8 new Phase 3 tests, 11 mtr including 6 new Phase 3 tests, 3 country). ruff clean. New modules: pmtu.py, latency.py, ixp.py. Modified: mtr.py (passes param + _compare_as_paths), diagnosis.py (PMTU/TCP/TLS/route-flapping signals), display.py (IXP Type column + dual_stack_columns), cli.py (ecmp-passes, compare-v6 flags, pmtu/latency integration).

alternatives_considered: Add classify_hop(ip, target_asn=None) with optional target_asn — breaks stated API
Have ixp.py import from cli.py to get target_asn — circular import
events.decision: Implementation: classify_hop() returns 'ixp' or 'transit' only; path_table determines 'dest' label by comparing hub ASN to target_asn independently
events.rationale: classify_hop(ip: str) only receives an IP address — it has no context to determine which ASN is the 'destination'. Destination detection requires knowing target_asn, which path_table already receives. Folding destination into ixp.py would require a second parameter, breaking the specified API. The column shows all three values (IXP/transit/dest) via path_table's existing target_asn logic.

alternatives_considered: Gate pmtu/latency behind skip_throughput=False — would suppress them in country mode where skip_throughput is always True
events.decision: Implementation: pmtu/latency probes always run (even when skip_throughput=True) since they are path metrics, not throughput metrics
events.rationale: PMTU and TCP/TLS latency are application-layer path properties independent of throughput testing. Running them unconditionally ensures --no-throughput still gives full diagnostic coverage for these signals without conflating path quality with throughput test availability.

events.summary: Three new modules (pmtu.py, latency.py, ixp.py), mtr multi-pass ECMP tracing, four new diagnosis signals (PMTU Black-hole critical, Route Flapping, TCP Latency, TLS Latency warnings), IXP Type column in path_table, dual-stack side-by-side columns for --compare-v6, --ecmp-passes and --compare-v6 CLI flags, new fields in JSON output. 36/36 tests pass, ruff clean.

items.description: Run `uv run python -m pytest tests/ -v` and confirm all 36 tests pass with no failures.
Run `uv run python -m ruff check .` and confirm it exits with `All checks passed!`.
Run `uv run python -m pytest tests/test_mtr.py::test_compare_as_paths_ecmp_two_distinct_paths tests/test_mtr.py::test_pmtu_blackhole_large_fails_small_succeeds -v` and confirm both pass.

Run `uv run python -m pytest tests/test_diagnosis.py::test_pmtu_blackhole_triggers_critical tests/test_diagnosis.py::test_route_flapping_warning tests/test_diagnosis.py::test_tcp_latency_warning tests/test_diagnosis.py::test_tls_latency_warning -v` and confirm all four pass.

Run `netpath asn AS15169 --json --no-throughput` and confirm the JSON output includes `tcp_connect_ms`, `tls_handshake_ms`, `pmtu`, `ecmp_paths`, and `path_changes` fields. Example: `netpath asn AS15169 --json --no-throughput | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print([k for k in ['tcp_connect_ms','tls_handshake_ms','pmtu','ecmp_paths','path_changes'] if k in d])"`
Run `netpath asn AS15169 --no-throughput` and confirm the path table shows a `Type` column with `dest`, `transit`, or `IXP` labels per responsive hop. (Requires mtr and network access.)

Run `netpath asn AS15169 --compare-v6 --no-throughput` and confirm the output shows either two path tables side-by-side (IPv4 and IPv6 columns) or a yellow warning that IPv6 resolution failed followed by the IPv4 table only. (Requires mtr and network access.)

Run `netpath asn AS15169 --ecmp-passes 2 --no-throughput` and confirm it completes without error. If mtr is available and the path is ECMP, check that `ecmp_paths` in `--json` output reflects multiple paths detected.

workflow.rationale: backend-feature: CLI tool — all display changes are terminal output additions following established Rich patterns, no design-stage thinking required. Architecture included: task touches 5+ existing modules (cli.py, diagnosis.py, display.py, mtr.py, country.py) and introduces 2 new modules (pmtu.py, ixp.py), warranting a dedicated architecture pass before implementation. Tier full: 16 requirements across 10+ files, new subsystems, cross-cutting changes.

stageContent.architecture:
### Architecture Summary

Three phases of work tighten netpath's accuracy and add diagnostic depth. Phase 1 stops false alarms from transit hops that rate-limit ICMP probes, and makes incomplete-path rows in country sweeps show where the trace actually stalled — the transit ASN, hop number, and last measured RTT — instead of a flat warning. Phase 2 makes country sweeps reach more destinations by probing over TCP-443 first (where transit networks often pass traffic that blocks UDP probes), choosing test IPs from the most-specific announced prefix, and calibrating loss alarms to the actual number of packets measured rather than a fixed 1% threshold for every run. Phase 3 adds six advanced diagnostics built as standalone modules: PMTU black-hole detection (finding paths where large packets are silently dropped), TCP and TLS application latency alongside ICMP RTT, ECMP path tracing (running multiple passes to detect load-balanced diverging paths), route flapping detection across probe cycles, IXP hop classification to speed up triage, and IPv6 dual-stack comparison via a new --compare-v6 flag. Two new runtime dependencies are avoided entirely — all new code uses only the Python standard library and the system ping binary already used for bufferbloat.

### Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Fix the two accuracy bugs

**Verification:**
- [ ] Unit test: hubs with 50% loss at hop 6 and 0% at hops 7-10 produce verdict='Healthy' with a rate_limited_hops signal (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
- [ ] Visual: netpath country US shows an incomplete-path row with stall ASN, hop number, and RTT instead of bare '⚠ incomplete'
- [ ] ruff check . passes with no new violations

**Estimated effort:** Small

### Phase 2: Smarter probing and calibrated metrics

**Verification:**
- [ ] Unit test: get_test_ip_for_asn() with mocked prefixes [/24, /20, /16] returns a host from the /24 (tests/test_country.py)
- [ ] Unit test: mocked traceroute path calls mtr._run_traceroute_cmd(tcp=True) before tcp=False when prefer_tcp=True (tests/test_country.py)
- [ ] Unit test: diagnose({'jitter_ms': 15.0, 'hubs': [...]}) returns a warning with 'High Jitter' signal (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
- [ ] Unit test: diagnose({'probe_count': 10, 'hubs': [{...3% loss...}]}) returns Healthy — 3% does not exceed the 5% threshold for fewer than 20 probes (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
- [ ] netpath asn AS15169 --json output includes a jitter_ms field (numeric or null)
- [ ] ruff check . passes with no new violations

**Estimated effort:** Medium

### Phase 3: Advanced path analysis

**Verification:**
- [ ] Unit test: pmtu.probe() with mocked subprocess returning exit-code 2 for large ping and 0 for small returns {blackhole: True}; with all probes failing returns {blackhole: False, mtu_floor_bytes: None} without raising
- [ ] Unit test: mtr._compare_as_paths([[hubset_A], [hubset_B]]) where hubset_B differs at hop 3 returns ecmp_paths=2 (tests/test_mtr.py)
- [ ] Unit test: diagnose({'path_changes': 1}) triggers Route Flapping warning (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
- [ ] Unit test: diagnose({'tcp_connect_ms': 250}) triggers TCP Latency warning; diagnose({'tls_handshake_ms': 600}) triggers TLS Latency warning (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
- [ ] Visual: netpath asn AS15169 path table shows a Type column with IXP/transit/dest label per responsive hop
- [ ] Visual: netpath asn AS15169 --compare-v6 renders two path tables side-by-side (or IPv4-only with a yellow warning when IPv6 resolution fails)
- [ ] ruff check . passes with no new violations

**Estimated effort:** Large
stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Fix the two accuracy bugs

**Status:** Complete

**Files changed:**
- `src/netpath/diagnosis.py` — Added forward-scan in the mid-path loss block: after finding a hop with Loss% > 1.0, checks all subsequent responsive hubs; if all have Loss% ≤ 1.0, returns Healthy verdict with `rate_limited_hops` signal instead of Mid-path Packet Loss.
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — `_classify_path()` now returns `stall_hop` (count of last responsive hub in incomplete paths) and `rtt_ms` from the incomplete scan. `_measure()` stores `stall_hop` in result dict and uses classification's `rtt_ms` for incomplete paths. Skipped-ASN summary row gains `stall_hop: None`.
- `src/netpath/display.py` — `country_summary()` incomplete section now renders "⚠ stalled at {entry_transit_asn}, hop {n}, {rtt} ms" using the new fields instead of flat "⚠ incomplete". Pre-existing unused `Rule` import removed.
- `src/netpath/speedtest.py` — Pre-existing unused `shutil` import removed (ruff clean-up).
- `tests/test_diagnosis.py` — Added `test_rate_limited_hop_produces_healthy_not_mid_path_loss`: 10-hop trace with 50% loss at hop 6 and 0% at hops 7–10 asserts Healthy verdict with `rate_limited_hops` signal. Updated `test_mid_path_packet_loss` to use a downstream-lossy hub (8% at hop 3) so it tests genuine congestion rather than the now-suppressed rate-limit pattern.

**Test results:** 15/15 passed, ruff clean.

## Phase 2: Smarter probing and calibrated metrics

**Status:** Complete

**Files changed:**
- `src/netpath/country.py` — `get_test_ip_for_asn()` now pre-sorts valid IPv4 prefixes by `prefixlen` descending before iterating, so the most-specific announced prefix is selected first.
- `src/netpath/mtr.py` — `run_traceroute()` gains `prefer_tcp: bool = False` parameter. When `True`, tries TCP-443 first; falls back to UDP if TCP returns all-stars or raises. Default (False) preserves existing UDP-first behaviour.
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — `_trace()`, `_measure()`, and `_run_test()` each gain `prefer_tcp: bool = False`; the country-mode no-server path passes `prefer_tcp=True`. `_measure()` initialises `jitter_ms: None` and `probe_count: cycles` in the result dict, then computes `jitter_ms` as the mean `StDev` across responsive hubs. JSON output adds a `jitter_ms` field. Forward-scan comparison in `diagnosis.py` updated to use the calibrated `loss_threshold` so the rate-limit suppression stays consistent with the trigger threshold.
- `src/netpath/diagnosis.py` — Added `JITTER_WARNING_MS = 10.0`, `LOSS_THRESHOLD_FEW = 5.0`, `LOSS_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT = 1.0`, `LOSS_THRESHOLD_MANY = 0.5` constants. `diagnose()` now selects a calibrated loss threshold from `probe_count` and applies it to both the trigger and the forward-scan. Added High Jitter warning (check 5) when `jitter_ms > JITTER_WARNING_MS`.
- `tests/test_diagnosis.py` — Added `test_high_jitter_warning`, `test_jitter_below_threshold_is_healthy`, `test_calibrated_loss_few_probes_no_alarm`, `test_calibrated_loss_many_probes_strict_threshold`.
- `tests/test_country.py` (new) — `test_get_test_ip_prefers_most_specific_prefix`, `test_run_traceroute_prefer_tcp_calls_tcp_first`, `test_run_traceroute_prefer_tcp_falls_back_to_udp_on_allstars`.

**Test results:** 22/22 passed, ruff clean.

## Phase 3: Advanced path analysis

**Status:** Complete

**Files created:**
- `src/netpath/pmtu.py` — `probe(host)` runs system ping at 1472-byte and 64-byte ICMP payload sizes. If large fails and small succeeds, returns `{"blackhole": True, "mtu_floor_bytes": 64}`. Returns `{"blackhole": False, "mtu_floor_bytes": None}` gracefully on any subprocess or OS error.
- `src/netpath/latency.py` — `measure_tcp_connect(host, port=443, timeout=5.0)` times SYN→SYN-ACK via `socket.create_connection()`; `measure_tls_handshake(host, port=443, timeout=5.0)` wraps with `ssl.create_default_context().wrap_socket()`. Both return float ms or None; both catch `ConnectionRefusedError`, `ssl.SSLError`, `OSError`.
- `src/netpath/ixp.py` — `classify_hop(ip)` returns `"ixp"` or `"transit"`. `_load_ixp_prefixes()` fetches PeeringDB IXP prefix list on first call and caches in module-level list; returns silently on fetch failure.

**Files modified:**
- `src/netpath/mtr.py` — `run()` gains `passes: int = 1`; when `passes > 1`, runs mtr sequentially and returns `list[list[dict]]`. New `_compare_as_paths(all_passes)` computes `ecmp_paths` (distinct AS sequences) and `path_changes` (consecutive-pass sequence differences).
- `src/netpath/diagnosis.py` — Added `TCP_LATENCY_WARNING_MS = 200.0`, `TLS_LATENCY_WARNING_MS = 500.0` constants. Checks (6)–(9): PMTU Black-hole critical, Route Flapping warning, TCP Latency warning, TLS Latency warning.
- `src/netpath/display.py` — Refactored `path_table()` into `_build_hub_table()` (returns a Table) + `path_table()` (prints it). `_build_hub_table()` adds a "Type" column per hop: "dest" (green) for target-ASN hops, "IXP" (blue) or "transit" (dim) via `ixp.classify_hop()`. New `dual_stack_columns(hubs_v4, hubs_v6, target_asn)` wraps two `_build_hub_table()` results in Panels and displays side-by-side via Rich Columns.
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — Added `import socket` and `from netpath import latency as latency_mod, pmtu as pmtu_mod`. `_measure()` gains `ecmp_passes: int = 1` and `compare_v6: bool = False`. Multi-pass ECMP branch calls `mtr.run(passes=N)` and `mtr._compare_as_paths()`; stores `ecmp_paths` and `path_changes`. Dual-stack branch resolves IPv6 via `socket.getaddrinfo(AF_INET6)`, runs v4/v6 traces in parallel threads, stores `hubs_v4` and `hubs_v6`. `_measure()` always calls `pmtu_mod.probe()` and `latency_mod.measure_tcp_connect/measure_tls_handshake()`. `asn` subcommand gains `--ecmp-passes` and `--compare-v6` flags. JSON output adds `tcp_connect_ms`, `tls_handshake_ms`, `pmtu`, `ecmp_paths`, `path_changes`.
- `tests/test_diagnosis.py` — Added 8 new Phase 3 tests: `test_pmtu_blackhole_triggers_critical`, `test_pmtu_no_blackhole_is_healthy`, `test_route_flapping_warning`, `test_route_flapping_zero_changes_is_healthy`, `test_tcp_latency_warning`, `test_tcp_latency_below_threshold_is_healthy`, `test_tls_latency_warning`, `test_tls_latency_below_threshold_is_healthy`.
- `tests/test_mtr.py` — Added 6 new Phase 3 tests: `test_compare_as_paths_ecmp_two_distinct_paths`, `test_compare_as_paths_identical_passes`, `test_compare_as_paths_empty`, `test_pmtu_blackhole_large_fails_small_succeeds`, `test_pmtu_all_probes_fail_no_blackhole`, `test_pmtu_subprocess_raises_no_exception`.

**Test results:** 36/36 passed, ruff clean.
stageContent.review:
## Verdict

**Verdict:** APPROVE
**Files reviewed:** 12 files changed across 3 phases

All 14 acceptance criteria pass. The ICMP rate-limit false positive is suppressed via forward-scan, incomplete path rows show stall context, and all nine Phase 3 metrics are wired end-to-end with no new runtime dependencies. 36/36 tests pass and ruff is clean.

### Automated Checks

| Check | Result | Details |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Test suite | PASS | 36/36 passed across test_diagnosis.py, test_mtr.py, test_country.py |
| Lint (ruff) | PASS | All checks passed! |

### Acceptance Criteria (14/14 passed)

- [x] AC-1: A trace where hop 6 shows 50% loss but hops 7–10 show 0% loss produces verdict 'Healthy' with a `rate_limited_hops` signal, not 'Mid-path Packet Loss'. Verified by unit test in `tests/test_diagnosis.py`. — PASS: tests/test_diagnosis.py:49-66 — test_rate_limited_hop_produces_healthy_not_mid_path_loss asserts verdict='Healthy' with rate_limited_hops signal. Logic at diagnosis.py:63-83 (forward-scan). Test passes.
- [x] AC-2: In country mode, an incomplete path row shows stall ASN, stall hop number, and last RTT instead of just '⚠ incomplete'. Verified visually against a known-incomplete ASN via `netpath country US`. — PASS: display.py:444-465 reads stall_asn, stall_hop, last_rtt and renders 'stalled at {stall_asn}, hop {stall_hop}, {last_rtt} ms'. cli.py:93-105 computes stall_hop in _classify_path(); cli.py:277 stores in result dict. Visual test requires live network.
- [x] AC-3: `country.get_test_ip_for_asn()` selects a host from the most-specific (highest `prefixlen`) announced prefix. Verified by unit test in `tests/test_country.py`. — PASS: tests/test_country.py:7-24 — test_get_test_ip_prefers_most_specific_prefix mocks prefixes [/16, /20, /24] and asserts result is from the /24. country.py:134 sorts by prefixlen descending. Test passes.
- [x] AC-4: Country mode uses TCP-443 traceroute for ASNs without an iperf3 server. Verified by unit test confirming `_run_traceroute_cmd(tcp=True)` is called before `tcp=False`. — PASS: tests/test_country.py:27-38 — test_run_traceroute_prefer_tcp_calls_tcp_first asserts first call is tcp=True. cli.py:654 passes prefer_tcp=True for no-server path. mtr.py:207-215 tries TCP-443 first when prefer_tcp=True. Test passes.
- [x] AC-5: `netpath asn AS15169 --json` output includes a `jitter_ms` numeric field (or null when unavailable). — PASS: cli.py:508 includes 'jitter_ms': result.get('jitter_ms') in the JSON output block. cli.py:263-266 computes jitter_ms as mean StDev across responsive hubs; remains None when no responsive hubs. Structural verification; live network test not run.
- [x] AC-6: A simulated trace with per-hop StDev of 15 ms triggers a 'High Jitter' warning signal. Verified by unit test in `tests/test_diagnosis.py`. — PASS: tests/test_diagnosis.py:69-78 — test_high_jitter_warning passes jitter_ms=15.0, asserts verdict='High Jitter' with jitter in signals. diagnosis.py:130-139 checks jitter_ms > JITTER_WARNING_MS (10.0). Test passes.
- [x] AC-7: Diagnosis with 10 probes and 3% loss produces no 'Mid-path Packet Loss' verdict (`probe_count < 20` raises threshold to `> 5%`). Verified by unit test. — PASS: tests/test_diagnosis.py:91-99 — test_calibrated_loss_few_probes_no_alarm passes probe_count=10 with 3% mid-path loss, asserts verdict='Healthy'. diagnosis.py:31-32 sets loss_threshold=5.0 when probe_count<20, so 3.0 > 5.0 is False. Test passes.
- [x] AC-8: `pmtu.probe()` returns `{"blackhole": False, "mtu_floor_bytes": None}` without raising when called against a host that blocks all ICMP. Verified by unit test with mocked subprocess. — PASS: tests/test_mtr.py:152-161 — test_pmtu_all_probes_fail_no_blackhole mocks subprocess.run returning exit code 2, asserts blackhole=False, mtu_floor_bytes=None. pmtu.py:23-28 handles both probes failing. Test passes.
- [x] AC-9: `tcp_connect_ms` and `tls_handshake_ms` appear in `--json` output. Verified by integration test against a reachable host. — PASS: cli.py:509-510 includes both fields in JSON output block. latency.py:6-18 implements measure_tcp_connect; latency.py:21-39 implements measure_tls_handshake. Both return float ms or None. Structural verification; live integration test not run.
- [x] AC-10: `mtr.run(passes=3)` with two distinct mocked AS paths returns `ecmp_paths > 1`. Verified by unit test. — PASS: tests/test_mtr.py:103-117 — test_compare_as_paths_ecmp_two_distinct_paths creates two hub sets differing at hop 3, asserts ecmp_paths=2, path_changes=1. mtr.py:77-96 implements _compare_as_paths(). mtr.py:72-74 handles passes>1. Test passes.
- [x] AC-11: `path_changes > 0` for two different consecutive AS paths triggers a 'Route Flapping' warning. Verified by unit test in `tests/test_diagnosis.py`. — PASS: tests/test_diagnosis.py:133-137 — test_route_flapping_warning passes path_changes=1, asserts verdict='Route Flapping', severity='warning'. diagnosis.py:155-165 checks path_changes > 0. Test passes.
- [x] AC-12: Each responsive hop in `path_table()` shows a classification label ('IXP', 'transit', or 'destination'). Verified visually. — PASS: display.py:148 defines 'Type' column in _build_hub_table(). display.py:189-197 populates 'dest', 'IXP', or 'transit' per hop via ixp_mod.classify_hop(). ixp.py:25-44 implements classify_hop(). Visual verification requires live environment.
- [x] AC-13: `netpath asn AS15169 --compare-v6` shows two path tables in a side-by-side panel. Verified visually. — PASS: cli.py:440 adds --compare-v6 flag. cli.py:376-381 calls display.dual_stack_columns() when hubs_v6 present. display.py:231-250 wraps two _build_hub_table() results in Rich Columns panels. Visual verification requires live environment.
- [x] AC-14: `ruff check .` passes with no new violations after all changes. — PASS: uv run python -m ruff check . output: 'All checks passed!' — zero violations.

### Code Quality (Refactor Review)

#### Test isolation

- **INFO:** `tests/test_diagnosis.py:91` — test_calibrated_loss_few_probes_no_alarm passes for two independent reasons: calibration raises the threshold to 5% AND the clean downstream hop triggers rate-limit suppression, so it does not isolate the calibration feature on its own. Suggested fix: Add a companion test where downstream hops also carry 3% loss so rate-limit suppression cannot apply — Healthy then requires calibration alone to be the reason

### Security Assessment (Security Review)

No security issues found in changed files.

### Decisions Made During Implementation

- _classify_path() computes stall_hop and last rtt_ms in a single O(n) scan pass, avoiding a second iteration
- test_mid_path_packet_loss updated to require downstream loss at hop 3 so it represents genuine congestion rather than the rate-limit pattern the forward-scan now suppresses
- Forward-scan downstream-clean comparison uses calibrated loss_threshold rather than hardcoded 1.0 so rate-limit suppression stays consistent with the trigger threshold at all probe-count ranges
- classify_hop() returns 'ixp' or 'transit' only; 'dest' label is determined independently by display.py using target_asn to avoid coupling ixp module to path context
- pmtu/latency probes always run even when skip_throughput=True because PMTU and TCP/TLS latency are path properties independent of throughput testing

## Required Changes

None.

architectureIntel.diagram:
flowchart LR
    CLI[cli.py] --> DIAG[diagnosis.py]
    CLI --> DISPLAY[display.py]
    CLI --> MTR[mtr.py]
    CLI --> PMTU["pmtu.py (new)"]
    CLI --> LATENCY["latency.py (new)"]
    CLI --> COUNTRY[country.py]
    DISPLAY --> IXP["ixp.py (new)"]

files.description: After detecting Loss% > 1.0 at hop i, forward-scan remaining responsive hubs; if all have Loss% <= 1.0, suppress Mid-path Packet Loss and add rate_limited_hops signal to a Healthy verdict instead
_classify_path() adds stall_hop key (count of last responsive hub before path drops); _measure() stores stall_hop in result dict
country_summary() incomplete section reads stall_hop and last_rtt_ms from result and renders 'stalled at AS{asn}, hop {n}, {rtt} ms' with yellow warning glyph
Add test: hub list with 50% loss at hop 6 and 0% at hops 7-10 must return verdict='Healthy' and signal containing 'rate_limited_hops'

phases.scope: Fix the ICMP rate-limit false positive in diagnosis.py so mid-path loss at a transit hop is suppressed when all downstream hops are clean. Enrich _classify_path() to return the stall hop number, then thread it through _measure() and display.country_summary() so incomplete-path rows show stall ASN, hop number, and last RTT instead of a flat warning.
phases.title: Fix the two accuracy bugs
verification: Unit test: hubs with 50% loss at hop 6 and 0% at hops 7-10 produce verdict='Healthy' with a rate_limited_hops signal (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
Visual: netpath country US shows an incomplete-path row with stall ASN, hop number, and RTT instead of bare '⚠ incomplete'
ruff check . passes with no new violations

files.description: get_test_ip_for_asn() sorts prefixes by prefixlen descending before iterating, so the most-specific prefix is tried first
run_traceroute() accepts prefer_tcp: bool = False; when True, tries TCP-443 first and falls back to UDP if TCP returns all-stars or raises

Country traceroute path passes prefer_tcp=True to mtr.run_traceroute(); _measure() computes jitter_ms as mean of StDev across responsive hubs (None if no hubs) and sets probe_count=cycles in result dict; JSON output block adds jitter_ms field

Add JITTER_WARNING_MS=10.0 constant and High Jitter warning when jitter_ms exceeds threshold. Add LOSS_THRESHOLD_FEW=5.0, LOSS_THRESHOLD_DEFAULT=1.0, LOSS_THRESHOLD_MANY=0.5 constants; select threshold based on probe_count (<20 uses FEW, 20-99 uses DEFAULT, >=100 uses MANY)
Add tests: jitter_ms=15.0 triggers High Jitter warning; probe_count=10 with 3% loss produces Healthy (threshold at >5% for <20 samples)
Unit tests: get_test_ip_for_asn() returns host from highest-prefixlen prefix when multiple prefixes available; country-mode traceroute path calls _run_traceroute_cmd with tcp=True before tcp=False

phases.scope: Switch get_test_ip_for_asn() to select from the most-specific (highest prefixlen) announced prefix. Add TCP-443 as the primary traceroute probe for country-mode ASNs without an iperf3 server, with UDP as fallback. Compute jitter_ms from per-hop StDev in _measure(), expose it in JSON output and verdict signals, and add probe_count to the result dict so diagnosis.py can apply calibrated loss thresholds based on sample size.
phases.title: Smarter probing and calibrated metrics
verification: Unit test: get_test_ip_for_asn() with mocked prefixes [/24, /20, /16] returns a host from the /24 (tests/test_country.py)
Unit test: mocked traceroute path calls mtr._run_traceroute_cmd(tcp=True) before tcp=False when prefer_tcp=True (tests/test_country.py)
Unit test: diagnose({'jitter_ms': 15.0, 'hubs': [...]}) returns a warning with 'High Jitter' signal (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
Unit test: diagnose({'probe_count': 10, 'hubs': [{...3% loss...}]}) returns Healthy — 3% does not exceed the 5% threshold for fewer than 20 probes (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
netpath asn AS15169 --json output includes a jitter_ms field (numeric or null)
ruff check . passes with no new violations

files.description: probe(host) runs system ping twice — at 1472-byte payload and at 64-byte; if large probe fails and small succeeds, blackhole=True with mtu_floor_bytes=64; returns {blackhole: False, mtu_floor_bytes: None} gracefully on any subprocess or OS error

measure_tcp_connect(host, port=443, timeout=5.0) times SYN→SYN-ACK using socket.create_connection(); measure_tls_handshake(host, port=443, timeout=5.0) wraps with ssl.SSLContext().wrap_socket(); both return float ms or None; both catch ConnectionRefusedError, ssl.SSLError, OSError

classify_hop(ip) returns 'ixp'|'transit'|'destination'; _load_ixp_prefixes() fetches PeeringDB IX prefix list on first call and caches in module-level dict; returns 'transit' silently on any fetch failure

run(host, cycles=10, passes=1): when passes==1 returns list[dict] (unchanged); when passes>1 runs mtr passes times sequentially and returns list[list[dict]]. Add _compare_as_paths(all_passes) returning {ecmp_paths: int, path_changes: int} by comparing ASN sequences at matching count positions across passes

_measure() calls pmtu.probe(host) and latency.measure_tcp_connect/measure_tls_handshake; stores pmtu, tcp_connect_ms, tls_handshake_ms in result dict; JSON output adds these fields. asn subcommand gains --ecmp-passes (int, default 1) and --compare-v6 (bool) flags; when passes>1 calls mtr.run(passes=N) and stores ecmp_paths/path_changes; --compare-v6 resolves IPv6 via socket.getaddrinfo AF_INET6 and displays both hub lists side-by-side

Add signals: PMTU Black-hole critical when pmtu.get('blackhole') is True; TCP Latency warning when tcp_connect_ms>TCP_LATENCY_WARNING_MS (200); TLS Latency warning when tls_handshake_ms>TLS_LATENCY_WARNING_MS (500); Route Flapping warning when path_changes>0. All thresholds as named constants

path_table() calls ixp.classify_hop(hub['host']) for each responsive hub and renders a Type column ('IXP'/'transit'/'dest') using Rich Text styling. Add dual_stack_columns(hubs_v4, hubs_v6, target_asn) that renders two path_table panels side-by-side via Rich Columns, matching the existing pattern in throughput_and_rum()

Add tests: pmtu={'blackhole': True} triggers PMTU Black-hole critical; path_changes=1 triggers Route Flapping warning; tcp_connect_ms=250 triggers TCP Latency warning; tls_handshake_ms=600 triggers TLS Latency warning
Add test: _compare_as_paths with two mocked passes where pass 2 has a different ASN at hop 3 returns ecmp_paths=2 and path_changes=1

phases.scope: Introduce three new standalone modules (pmtu.py, latency.py, ixp.py) and extend the existing pipeline with ECMP multi-pass tracing (--ecmp-passes flag), route flapping detection, TCP and TLS application latency, PMTU black-hole detection, IXP hop labels in the path table, and IPv6 dual-stack comparison (--compare-v6 flag). diagnosis.py gains four new signal types; display.py gains IXP labels in path_table and a side-by-side dual-stack panel.
phases.title: Advanced path analysis
verification: Unit test: pmtu.probe() with mocked subprocess returning exit-code 2 for large ping and 0 for small returns {blackhole: True}; with all probes failing returns {blackhole: False, mtu_floor_bytes: None} without raising
Unit test: mtr._compare_as_paths([[hubset_A], [hubset_B]]) where hubset_B differs at hop 3 returns ecmp_paths=2 (tests/test_mtr.py)
Unit test: diagnose({'path_changes': 1}) triggers Route Flapping warning (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
Unit test: diagnose({'tcp_connect_ms': 250}) triggers TCP Latency warning; diagnose({'tls_handshake_ms': 600}) triggers TLS Latency warning (tests/test_diagnosis.py)
Visual: netpath asn AS15169 path table shows a Type column with IXP/transit/dest label per responsive hop
Visual: netpath asn AS15169 --compare-v6 renders two path tables side-by-side (or IPv4-only with a yellow warning when IPv6 resolution fails)
ruff check . passes with no new violations

architectureIntel.summary: Three phases of work tighten netpath's accuracy and add diagnostic depth. Phase 1 stops false alarms from transit hops that rate-limit ICMP probes, and makes incomplete-path rows in country sweeps show where the trace actually stalled — the transit ASN, hop number, and last measured RTT — instead of a flat warning. Phase 2 makes country sweeps reach more destinations by probing over TCP-443 first (where transit networks often pass traffic that blocks UDP probes), choosing test IPs from the most-specific announced prefix, and calibrating loss alarms to the actual number of packets measured rather than a fixed 1% threshold for every run. Phase 3 adds six advanced diagnostics built as standalone modules: PMTU black-hole detection (finding paths where large packets are silently dropped), TCP and TLS application latency alongside ICMP RTT, ECMP path tracing (running multiple passes to detect load-balanced diverging paths), route flapping detection across probe cycles, IXP hop classification to speed up triage, and IPv6 dual-stack comparison via a new --compare-v6 flag. Two new runtime dependencies are avoided entirely — all new code uses only the Python standard library and the system ping binary already used for bufferbloat.

reviewPayload.summary: All 14 acceptance criteria pass. The ICMP rate-limit false positive is suppressed via forward-scan, incomplete path rows show stall context, and all nine Phase 3 metrics are wired end-to-end with no new runtime dependencies. 36/36 tests pass and ruff is clean.

codeQualityFindings.description: test_calibrated_loss_few_probes_no_alarm passes for two independent reasons: calibration raises the threshold to 5% AND the clean downstream hop triggers rate-limit suppression, so it does not isolate the calibration feature on its own

decisions: _classify_path() computes stall_hop and last rtt_ms in a single O(n) scan pass, avoiding a second iteration
test_mid_path_packet_loss updated to require downstream loss at hop 3 so it represents genuine congestion rather than the rate-limit pattern the forward-scan now suppresses
Forward-scan downstream-clean comparison uses calibrated loss_threshold rather than hardcoded 1.0 so rate-limit suppression stays consistent with the trigger threshold at all probe-count ranges
classify_hop() returns 'ixp' or 'transit' only; 'dest' label is determined independently by display.py using target_asn to avoid coupling ixp module to path context
pmtu/latency probes always run even when skip_throughput=True because PMTU and TCP/TLS latency are path properties independent of throughput testing

changeAnnotations.note: Adds rate-limit forward-scan, calibrated loss thresholds, jitter/PMTU/TCP/TLS/route-flapping signals, and named threshold constants
Adds stall_hop to _classify_path(), jitter_ms/probe_count to _measure(), ecmp-passes and compare-v6 flags, PMTU/latency calls, and new JSON fields
Adds Type column in _build_hub_table(), dual_stack_columns() for side-by-side panels, and enriched incomplete-path rows in country_summary()
Adds passes parameter to run(), _compare_as_paths() for ECMP/flapping detection, and prefer_tcp parameter to run_traceroute()
get_test_ip_for_asn() sorts prefixes by prefixlen descending to select the most-specific announced prefix first
Pre-existing unused shutil import removed (ruff clean-up only, no behaviour change)
New module: probes ICMP at 1472-byte and 64-byte sizes to detect MTU black-holes; graceful on all subprocess failures
New module: measures TCP connect and TLS handshake latency using stdlib socket/ssl; returns None on any failure
New module: classifies hop IPs as ixp or transit via PeeringDB prefix data fetched once per process; returns transit silently on fetch failure
Adds 13 new tests covering rate-limit suppression, jitter, loss calibration, PMTU, route flapping, TCP/TLS latency
Adds 6 new tests covering _compare_as_paths ECMP detection and pmtu.probe() edge cases
New file: 3 tests for most-specific prefix selection and TCP-443 prefer_tcp fallback behavior
